entities.name: PLANT GENETIC RESOURCES FOR FOOD AND AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Genetic materials of plant origin of actual or potential value for food and agriculture, vital for sustainable agriculture and food security

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agricultural practices that ensure long-term food production without environmental degradation

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: EX-SITU GENEBANKS
entities.description: Facilities that conserve biological diversity outside their natural habitats, serving as repositories for crop diversity

entities.name: CGIAR GENEBANKS
entities.description: A network of genebanks under the Consultative Group on International Agricultural Research that conserve crop, forage, and tree species

entities.name: SVALBARD GLOBAL SEED VAULT
entities.description: A secure seed storage facility in Norway used for the safety duplication of global crop diversity

entities.name: LONG TERM STORAGE CONDITION
entities.description: Storage conditions designed for the permanent preservation of germplasm

entities.name: ICRISAT GENEBANK
entities.description: One of the largest repositories in the CGIAR system, conserving over 131,000 accessions of 11 crops and their wild relatives

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop with extensive accessions, landraces, and wild species conserved for genetic diversity

entities.name: WILD SPECIES
entities.description: Non-domesticated relatives of crop species that serve as reservoirs of useful genes

entities.name: NATIONAL GENEBANK OF INDIA
entities.description: The national repository in India conserving groundnut accessions under various storage conditions

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the National genebank of India is located

entities.name: MEDIUM-TERM STORAGE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Storage conditions for germplasm that require periodic regeneration

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs aimed at enhancing crop production, yield, and resistance through the use of genetic resources

entities.name: CORE SUBSET
entities.description: A representative subset of a larger germplasm collection used to promote resource utilization

entities.name: MINI CORE SUBSET
entities.description: A further reduced representative subset of a core collection used for genomic studies

entities.name: RUST
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting crops, used as a target for developing trait-specific subsets

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting groundnut, used as a target for developing trait-specific subsets

entities.name: TRAIT-SPECIFIC SUBSETS
entities.description: Subsets of germplasm selected for specific desirable traits such as disease resistance

entities.name: SSR-BASED GENOTYPING
entities.description: A genotyping method using Simple Sequence Repeats to identify genetic diversity and form reference sets

entities.name: REFERENCE SET
entities.description: A curated set of accessions used as a standard for genetic comparison and analysis

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION STUDIES
entities.description: An analytical approach used to identify genetic markers associated with specific traits in a population

entities.name: MTAS
entities.description: Marker-Trait Associations that link specific genetic markers to phenotypic traits

entities.name: FRESH SEED DORMANCY
entities.description: A trait related to the inability of freshly harvested seeds to germinate

entities.name: STEM ROT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to resist infection by stem rot pathogens

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics of a crop that determine its nutritional value

entities.name: ALLELE MINING
entities.description: The process of identifying and extracting useful alleles from genebank diversity to accelerate crop improvement

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Environmental shifts that necessitate the acceleration of crop improvement to enhance production

relationships.source: PLANT GENETIC RESOURCES FOR FOOD AND AGRICULTURE

relationships.target: PLANT GENETIC RESOURCES FOR FOOD AND AGRICULTURE
